name: Bishop Dr. Fekadu Ayele Shone
role: Bishop Dr. Fekadu Ayele Shone is the National Overseer for Ethiopia & Djibouti for the Church of God of Prophecy

biography.p1: He was born on August 20, 1954, in Gudaya Jare District, Wollega, Oromia, Ethiopia. He was converted in February 1974, while 19 years old, and filled and Baptized by the Holy Ghost the same year. Most of his family and relatives have been believers since his conversion. He Married Beirut Deressa Mamede on May 7, 1978. The Lord has blessed them with six boys, two girls, three grandsons, and five granddaughters. Most of his family serve in Church work and evangelistic campaigns, doing more in support of his ministry.
biography.p2: Bishop Dr. Fekadu founded The Ethiopian Living Word Evangelical Church with the guidance of divine instruction given to him in October 1975. After sixteen years, on September 18, 1991, officially announced. In 1996, on March 4, he officially joined The Church of God of Prophecy and Ordained Bishop. Under his leadership, the ministry has expanded to over 84 congregations nationwide. His evangelistic experience, pastoral judgment, and commitment to leadership development have strengthened the Church at every level.
biography.p3: Bishop Dr. Fekadu speaks three languages fluently: Amharic, Oromiffa, and English. He also strives to maintain relationships with congregations all over the world. Bishop Dr. Fekadu has earned several advanced degrees, including Video & Media Production at Shoreline Community College in 2010 in the United States, an Advanced Diploma at Evangelical Theological College in 1992 in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, an AAT/IT network Security/Administration at North Seattle Community College in 2009 in the United States, a bachelor’s degree in Bible and Theology from Global University in 2008 in the United States, a master’s degree in organizational management from Ashford University in 2010 in the United States, and his Doctorate of Transformational Leadership from Bakke Graduate University in 2016 in the United States. He seeks to honor the Lord with the opportunities he has been given to build God's kingdom through the ministry of reconciliation and restoration.
